sections.content: Retry logic looks harmless. Until it duplicates money transfers, orders, or state changes. Network failures make clients retry, and servers process the same request twice.
Without idempotency, payments double charge, records duplicate, and inventory becomes inconsistent. The system appears healthy while silently corrupting data.
Every state-changing operation must have idempotency keys, request deduplication, and safe retry semantics. If repeating a request changes results, your system is unsafe.
Retries increase reliability only when operations are idempotent. Otherwise, retries amplify failure.
